provenance.role: Dated, replaceable lookup mapping each orchestration capability class (orchestration/roles.json) to the concrete model-version bindings available to it, per binding kind {claude-code-subagent | worker-provider | human | deterministic-tool}.
faithful_to: docs/CODEX_ADAPTER.md §1 (model names are dated, non-load-bearing, capability-classes-only in Ring-0)
provenance.sole_model_name_home: This is the ONLY sanctioned home for model-version-name strings in the DMC control plane. Model names must never appear in Ring-0 code, role logic, or gate/verdict paths. The v0.6.1 / M8 model-name self-scan EXCLUDES this file by design (the dated row of docs/CODEX_ADAPTER.md §1 is the parallel precedent).
provenance.not_load_bearing: No gate, verdict, routing, or delegation-execution decision reads this file. `dmc doctor` may DISPLAY it. It is a lookup/reference artifact only; replace it as models evolve and re-date `as_of`.
provenance.date_semantics: `as_of` pins the freshness of the bindings below. A stale date is a signal to refresh, never a gate failure.

frontier-long-horizon.note: Strategic Orchestrator lane — deepest reasoning / longest horizon; main-session bound, never a mutator.

standard-implementation.note: Implementer lane — the only mutation-capable role; executor subagent mutates under a scope.lock, worker providers are proposal-only.

cheap-fast.note: High-frequency lightweight lane — quick lookups, exploration, cheap checks.

adversarial-review.note: Critic / Falsifier and Release Auditor lanes — fresh-context adversarial review; advisory verdicts only (C11), never a release grant.

deterministic-tool.note: Verifier lane — deterministic Ring-0 tooling, not a model. Reports the deterministic result; never a model self-assessment.

human-only-gate.note: Human Release Gate — the only lane that may open a release/closure gate. Never inferred, never automated, never a model.
